dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: Security-enforcing table. The Product-level counterpart of WorkItemPolicy: it gates AI work anchored on a Product rather than on one work item — today, generating the work that achieves a Deliverable.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: What the AI is allowed to do with a whole Product — read its brief and strategy, and create work items — and through which provider.

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.rules: must reference a Product row; one policy per Product

entries.description: May the AI read this Product's brief, strategy, and deliverables?
May the AI create planning work items against this Product?

entries.rules: must reference an AIProvider row when set; no provider means blocked
entries.description: The only AI provider this Product's work may go to
How much effort the AI may spend on this Product's requests

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Kept with its Product. Setting a policy again replaces the previous row, so there is only ever one per Product.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?
sensitiveFields.answer: This table is itself a protection: it decides whether a Product's brief and strategy may be sent to an AI provider. No secret values are stored here — the provider's API key lives in the OS credential store.

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: Deny-by-default: a Product with no row here is closed to AI. Generation requires allowRead AND allowGenerate AND a providerId that still exists. One policy per Product; effortTier stays in its list. This policy is deliberately coarser than a work-item policy — allowing generation covers every Deliverable of the Product.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.
